title: Human Needs SDT FAIL: falsifier with wrong d_derived and wrong SigmaT sum
_fixture_purpose: Falsifier: declares a derivation witness where d_derived does not equal b+e (should trip HN_DERIV) and a sigma witness whose declared sums don't match the tuple sums (should trip HN_SIGMA). Everything else mirrors the PASS derivation-chain fixture so this isolates the two planted defects.

derivation_witnesses.label: fundamental QN (1,1,2,3)
derivation_witnesses.note: Fibonacci seed; generators (b=1,e=1) produce all derived quantities

derivation_witnesses.label: Lucas seed (2,1,3,4) -- DELIBERATELY WRONG d_derived
derivation_witnesses.note: Falsifier: d_derived should be 3 (b+e=2+1), fixture declares 999 to trip HN_DERIV

delta_witnesses.label: Fibonacci T-step derivative
delta_witnesses.note: Growth (DeltaT) preserves QA relations: delta-d = delta-b + delta-e

sigma_witnesses.label: Fibonacci 3-step summation -- DELIBERATELY WRONG declared sums
sigma_witnesses.check: sa should be 16 (sb+2*se=4+12=16), fixture declares 999 to trip HN_SIGMA
sigma_witnesses.note: Falsifier: declared sa does not match actual tuple sum nor sb+2*se

fundamental_theorem_witness.label: Sigma(DeltaT) = T(n) - T(0): contribution integrates growth
fundamental_theorem_witness.note: Sigma(DeltaT from T0 to T3) = T3 - T0. Left correct here so this fixture isolates the HN_DERIV and HN_SIGMA defects only.

source_grounding.note: DOI: 10.1177/19485506221113678 -- authors are Martela, Lehmus-Sun, Parker, Pessi & Ryan; Deci is not an author on this specific paper

fail_ledger: HN_DERIV: W_LUCAS d_derived declared 999, should be 3
HN_SIGMA: W_SIGMA_FIB declared sa=999, actual tuple sum and sb+2*se both give 16
